'''Winstel Greelanx''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] who served as an [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] in the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]].

==Biography==
In [[4 BBY/Legends|4 BBY]], Greelanx commanded an Imperial strike force ordered by [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Sarn Shild]] to devastate [[Nar Shaddaa/Legends|Nar Shaddaa]], as per the orders of [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] that the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Empire]] crack down on [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] activity. [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] was sent by [[Jiliac Desilijic Tiron|Jiliac]] and [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba]] to offer a bribe to Greelanx on behalf of all the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutts]] on [[Nal Hutta/Legends|Nal Hutta]] ''not'' to attack Nar Shaddaa. Greelanx could name his price, according to the Hutts. Rather than simply throw the battle, Greelanx gave to Solo a copy of his battle plans, allowing the [[Smuggler/Legends|smugglers]] to devise a reasonable defense strategy. Greelanx accepted the bribe in the form of precious gems so he could retire immediately after "invading" Nar Shaddaa.

A twist occurred when Greelanx received an order through the most secure channel, the channel used only by the Emperor himself, commanding Greelanx to feign defeat and allow Nar Shaddaa to survive. Puzzled by this turn of events, Greelanx attacked Nar Shaddaa with only a half-hearted effort, much to the shock and frustration of his subordinate, [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Soontir Fel/Legends|Soontir Fel]]. When the smugglers put up a surprisingly effective defense and destroyed numerous Imperial ships, Greelanx sounded the retreat and fled from [[Hutt Space/Legends|Hutt Space]].

After the [[Battle of Nar Shaddaa (4 BBY)|Battle of Nar Shaddaa]], Solo returned to Greelanx as promised and delivered the precious gems. However, before Solo could leave, one of Greelanx's superiors arrived and was furious at the battle's outcome. Solo listened in horror from inside a closet as Greelanx [[Telekinesis/Legends#Choking powers|suffocated]] at the hands of [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]].

[[Bria Tharen]] suspected Greelanx had been ordered to throw the battle by the Emperor so as to remove support from [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Sarn Shild]], who was becoming too power-hungry for Palpatine's liking. After Shild committed suicide, Vader was merely tying up a loose end.
